tldr: Migrate from Adyen's enterprise unified payments platform to 2Checkout for SaaS and e-commerce billing.
why_switch: Adyen's minimum fees are heavy for sub-$1M TPV; 2Checkout fits SaaS/e-commerce volumes
Want a checkout product oriented around digital goods and subscriptions out of the box
Reduce operational complexity of interchange++ negotiated pricing
Consolidate global tax/VAT handling that 2Checkout's merchant-of-record model can absorb
Simplify vendor relationship away from enterprise contracts
what_to_export: Stored payment methods / tokens (subject to PCI scope rules)
Webhook endpoint inventory and event types in use
API keys and integration credentials inventory
export_method: Use Adyen's CSV export and reporting tools in the Customer Area to pull recurring payment details, transactions, and payout reports. For tokenized cards, request a card data migration through Adyen support — networks generally require a PCI-compliant processor-to-processor transfer, not a self-serve download. Validate scope and timing with Adyen's official migration team before scheduling cutover.
import_method: Import customers, products, and subscriptions into 2Checkout via their merchant dashboard or API. Coordinate a PCI-compliant card token migration between Adyen and 2Checkout's vault provider; do not attempt to move raw PANs yourself. Re-point checkout flows and webhooks to 2Checkout endpoints, and run parallel processing for a billing cycle to validate. Confirm exact API and import endpoints in the current 2Checkout official docs.

concept_mapping.note: Map identifier and persist mapping table during cutover
Requires PCI-compliant token migration, not direct copy
Different session lifecycle — re-implement client integration
Event names and payload shapes differ
Confirm rates with sales — pricing is not publicly itemized

gotchas: Card token migration requires a PCI-compliant processor-to-processor transfer; plan with both vendors
2Checkout's GitHub SDKs show stale activity (last commit 1000+ days ago); validate SDK currency directly with 2Checkout support
2Checkout pricing is not transparent — get a written quote before committing
Merchant-of-record vs gateway models differ; tax and refund flows may change semantically
Subscription proration, trials, and dunning logic rarely map 1:1 — re-test every plan
Run dual processing for at least one full billing cycle before decommissioning Adyen

email.subject: Migration plan: Adyen to 2Checkout
email.body:
Hi team,

I'd like to move our payments from Adyen to 2Checkout. Adyen's enterprise model is heavier than we need at our current TPV, and 2Checkout's SaaS/e-commerce focus matches our use case better.

The critical piece is the PCI-compliant token transfer between vaults — that has to be coordinated with both vendors, not done by us. We will export customers, subscriptions, and reports from Adyen, import to 2Checkout, run parallel for one full billing cycle, then cut over. Estimated 3-8 weeks.

Before we commit, I want a written quote from 2Checkout (their pricing isn't public) and confirmation that their current SDKs are actively maintained.

Green light?
